sourceMovie.title: The Neon Demon
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Neon Demon (2016) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Nicolas Winding Refn's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: It Follows
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Neon Demon clicked for you, It Follows (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Neon Demon?
faq.answer: Try It Follows, The Substance, Nosferatu—each shares DNA with The Neon Demon while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Neon Demon?
faq.answer: The Neon Demon (2016) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Neon Demon worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
